query: Has Russia announced any extension of its gasoline (motor gasoline/benzine) export ban beyond July 31, 2026? Look for any cabinet resolution, decree, or official statement from late June 2026 or early July 2026 reported by TASS, Reuters, or government.ru. Also describe the recent status of Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian refineries and domestic gasoline shortages as of late June 2026, and historically how many days before each deadline past gasoline export ban extensions were formally announced.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is a summary of the key findings:

## Bottom Line

**No formal decree extending the gasoline export ban beyond July 31, 2026 had been signed as of July 1, 2026** — but the government has clearly telegraphed its intent to extend.

### Extension status
- The current **complete ban on gasoline and jet-fuel exports is in force through July 31, 2026** (per the April 2, 2026 decree, government.ru/news/58316).
- **June 23, 2026:** Government press statement said it is "seriously considering" extending gasoline/diesel/jet-fuel bans **until end-2026**.
- **June 29–30, 2026:** Putin reaffirmed the complete gasoline/kerosene export ban and created a monitoring headquarters under Novak.
- **Key caveat:** Much late-June news actually concerns a *separate* measure — the **EAEU zero fuel duty**, which the EEC Council formally extended to June 30, 2027 on June 30. That is a customs-duty measure, **not** the export ban.

### Historical timing of extension announcements
Russia consistently signs these extension decrees **very close to the deadline (within the final week to a few days)**, while Novak signals intent 2–4 weeks ahead via TASS/RIA:
- Dec 31, 2025 → Feb 28, 2026: decreed **late December 2025** (days before deadline)
- Feb 28 → July 31, 2026: signed **Jan 25–31, 2026** (~4 weeks ahead)
- A formal extension beyond July 31, 2026 would, by this pattern, most likely appear in **late July 2026**.

### Drone strikes & shortages (late June 2026)
- **50+ strikes** on refineries since late March; May set a war record of ~30 attacks. Moscow refinery (40% of capital fuel) hit repeatedly, repairs 3+ months.
- June crude processing **-25% y/y to 3.95M bpd** (20+ year low); gasoline output **-17% to ~850K bpd**; ~one-third of refining capacity offline.
- Shortages/rationing in **50+ regions** (Crimea/Sevastopol worst); wholesale prices AI-95 +33%, diesel +40% YTD. Putin admitted queues but called them "not critical." Russia arranging gasoline imports.

I've recorded all data points and 22 cited sources in the structured output.
